public boolean canTransform(String start, String end) {

    List<Character> sChars = new ArrayList<>();

    List<Integer> sPos = new ArrayList<>();

    for (int i=0; i < start.length(); i++) {

        char c = start.charAt(i);

        if (c != 'X') {

            sChars.add(c);

            sPos.add(i);

        }

    }

    List<Character> eChars = new ArrayList<>();

    List<Integer> ePos = new ArrayList<>();

    for (int i=0; i < end.length(); i++) {

        char c = end.charAt(i);

        if (c != 'X') {

            eChars.add(c);

            ePos.add(i);

        }

    }

    // Check if the non-X sequences are same

    if (sChars.size() != eChars.size()) return false;

    for (int i=0; i < sChars.size(); i++) {

        if (!sChars.get(i).equals(eChars.get(i))) {

            return false;

        }

    }

    // Now check positions for each character

    for (int i=0; i < sChars.size(); i++) {

        char c = sChars.get(i);

        int sPosVal = sPos.get(i);

        int ePosVal = ePos.get(i);

        if (c == 'L' && ePosVal > sPosVal) return false;

        if (c == 'R' && ePosVal < sPosVal) return false;

    }

    return true;

}

Now, time and space complexity.

Time complexity: O(n), where n is the length of the string. Because we process each character once to collect the non-X characters and positions, then check in O(k) time where k is the number of non-X characters (which is <=n). So overall O(n).

Space complexity: O(n) for storing the lists.

But in Java, the code for the solution would be:

class Solution {
    public boolean canTransform(String start, String end) {
        List<Character> sChars = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Integer> sPos = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < start.length(); i++) {
            char c = start.charAt(i);
            if (c != 'X') {
                sChars.add(c);
                sPos.add(i);
            }
        }
        List<Character> eChars = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Integer> ePos = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < end.length(); i++) {
            char c = end.charAt(i);
            if (c != 'X') {
                eChars.add(c);
                ePos.add(i);
            }
        }
        // Check if the non-X sequences are the same
        if (sChars.size() != eChars.size()) return false;
        for (int i = 0; i < sChars.size(); i++) {
            if (!sChars.get(i).equals(eChars.get(i))) return false;
        }
        // Check positions for each character
        for (int i = 0; i < sChars.size(); i++) {
            char c = sChars.get(i);
            int sPosVal = sPos.get(i);
            int ePosVal = ePos.get(i);
            if (c == 'L' && ePosVal > sPosVal) return false;
            if (c == 'R' && ePosVal < sPosVal) return false;
        }
        return true;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean canTransform(String start, String end) {
        List<Character> sChars = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Integer> sPos = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < start.length(); i++) {
            char c = start.charAt(i);
            if (c != 'X') {
                sChars.add(c);
                sPos.add(i);
            }
        }
        List<Character> eChars = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Integer> ePos = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < end.length(); i++) {
            char c = end.charAt(i);
            if (c != 'X') {
                eChars.add(c);
                ePos.add(i);
            }
        }
        if (sChars.size() != eChars.size()) return false;
        for (int i = 0; i < sChars.size(); i++) {
            if (!sChars.get(i).equals(eChars.get(i))) return false;
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < sChars.size(); i++) {
            char c = sChars.get(i);
            int sPosVal = sPos.get(i);
            int ePosVal = ePos.get(i);
            if (c == 'L' && ePosVal > sPosVal) return false;
            if (c == 'R' && ePosVal < sPosVal) return false;
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution filters out 'X' characters and compares the sequences of 'L' and 'R' characters between the start and end strings. It ensures that the order of non-'X' characters matches and verifies that each 'L' in the end string is not to the right of its position in the start string (as 'L's can only move left), and each 'R' is not to the left of its start position (as 'R's can only move right). This approach is efficient with linear time and space complexity.
